specialty.name: Avocado oil-washed tequila
specialty.recipe:
- 4 oz avocado oil
- 16 oz/475 mL blanco tequila

Combine tequila and oil in a glass container and let steep at room temperature for 4–6 hours. Transfer to freezer to solidify oil over night. Discard solidified oil and strain remaining liquid through a coffee filter. 

Makes ~6 oz/475 mL
Source: Tiki: Modern Tropical Cocktails

specialty.name: Avocado Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 525 mL filtered water
- 945 g sugar
- 945 mL lime juice
- 945 g soft avocado flesh

In a medium bowl, combine the water and sugar and stir until dissolved. Add the lime juice and avocados, blend with an immersion blender until smooth, and then strain through a chinois. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 2 days. 

MAKES 3.8 L. 
Source: NoMad.

specialty.name: Banana oleo saccharum
specialty.recipe: Macerate equal mass banana peels and cane sugar; let sit at room temperature, shaking occasionally, until sugar is completely dissolved' strain through nut milk bag or coffee filter to clarify

specialty.name: Banana Oloroso
specialty.recipe:
- 1 L Lustau Oloroso Sherry
- 4 overripe/black bananas, peeled
- 4 mg Pectin X

Blend together the sherry, bananas, and pectin. Add to a Spinzall, 375 mL at a time, and put on continuous until the liquid becomes clear, then strain through a chinois. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 1 L
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Basil Fennel Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 100 g basil leaves
- 100 g fennel, roughly chopped
- 800 g Simple Syrup (this page)
Brix: 48

Combine the basil, fennel, and simple syrup in an iSi canister. Charge it twice using N2O (cream) chargers, shaking the canister between each charge. Allow the canister to sit for 5 minutes and then vent by pushing the nozzle out quickly; place a container underneath the tip to catch any liquid that may be released. Unscrew the top of the canister. Once the liquid stops bubbling, strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter. Let the syrup cool to room temperature and store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 800 g
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Brown Butter Falernum
specialty.recipe:
300 g unsalted butter, cubed
1 L  Falernum

In a large pot over medium heat, melt the butter, whisking constantly, while the milk solids brown evenly. Continue to let brown, whisking, until the color is as dark as an almond skin. Remove from the heat and add the velvet falernum. Transfer the butter to a storage container and place in the freezer until the fat has risen to the top and solidified. Remove and discard the solidified fat cap. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES ~1 L
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Cacao Nib-Infused Amaro
specialty.recipe:
- 250 mL Amaro of choice
- 50 g cacao nibs
- 4 allspice berries, cracked
- 1–2 black cardamom pod, crushed
- 2–3 g dried shiitake or kombu

Combine everything in a 500 mL iSi and double charge, shaking betweek charges. Let infuse for 2–3 minutes, shaking occasionally. Depressurize and strain through a coffee filter.

specialty.name: Cacao Nib-Infused Brandy
specialty.recipe:
- 150 g cacao nibs
- 750 mL Pierre Ferrand 1840 Cognac

Combine the cacao nibs and Cognac in an iSi canister. Charge it twice using N2O (cream) chargers, shaking the canister between each charge. Allow the canister to sit for 5 minutes and then vent by pushing the nozzle out quickly; place a container underneath the tip to catch any liquid that may be released. Unscrew the top of the canister. Once the liquid stops bubbling, strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 750 ML
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Cacao Nib-Infused Tequila
specialty.description: Cold-infused blanco tequila with cacao nibs
specialty.recipe:
- 150 g cacao nibs
- 750 mL Blanco Tequila

Combine the cacao nibs and tequila in an iSi canister. Charge it twice using N2O (cream) chargers, shaking the canister between each charge. Allow the canister to sit for 5 minutes and then vent by pushing the nozzle out quickly; place a container underneath the tip to catch any liquid that may be released. Unscrew the top of the canister. Once the liquid stops bubbling, strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 750 ML
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Celery Root–Infused Blanc Vermouth
specialty.recipe:
- 500 g celery root, peeled and diced
- 750 mL Dolin de Chambery Blanc vermouth

Preheat the oven to 200°C/400°F and line a sheet pan with parchment paper. Place the celery root on the prepared sheet pan and roast until dark golden brown, 25 to 30 minutes. In a large bowl, combine the celery root and vermouth, let steep for 1 hour, and then strain through a chinois. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 8 weeks.

MAKES 750 ML
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Chai Infused Sweet Vermouth
specialty.recipe:
- 30 g chai tea
- 750 mL Cocchi Vermouth di Torino

In a large bowl, combine the tea and vermouth, let steep for 3 to 5 minutes
(taste as you go because it becomes tannic very quickly), and then strain
through a chinois. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 6
weeks.

MAKES 750 ML
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Chai-Turmeric Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 90 g chai tea
- 2.7 kg hot filtered water
- 2.7 kg sugar
- 3 g xanthan gum
- 30 g Arabic gum
- 15 g turmeric

In a large container, steep the tea in the hot water for 12 minutes. Pass the
liquid through a chinois, but do not to press the loose tea, as the liquid will be
bitter. Add the sugar and stir until fully dissolved, then transfer the mixture to
a high-speed blender. While blending, sift in the xanthan gum, then the
Arabic gum, and then the turmeric. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated,
for up to 1 month.

MAKES 4 L
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Chai-Turmeric Yogurt Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 500 mL Chai-Turmeric Syrup (preceding recipe)
- 500 mL unsweetened sheep’s milk yogurt

In a medium bowl, combine the syrup and yogurt and stir until fully
incorporated. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 1 L
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Chamomile Honey Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 25 g chamomile tea
- 225 g hot filtered water
- 350 g clover honey
Brix: 60

In a large container, steep the tea in the hot water for 3 minutes. Pass the
liquid through a chinois, but do not to press the loose tea, as the liquid will be
bitter. Add the honey and stir until fully dissolved. Let the syrup cool to room
temperature. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 575 G
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Charred Pineapple Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 1 pineapple, peeled, cored, and sliced into 1-inch planks
- Demerara Simple Syrup, as needed
- Plantation pineapple rum, as needed

On a stove-top plancha or griddle, char the pineapple planks until black on
both sides. Transfer the pineapple to an ovenproof container, cover with
ovenproof plastic wrap, and place the container in a metal sheet pan. Steam in
the combi oven for 30 minutes at 70°C/158°F and 100 percent humidity.
Transfer the contents to a high-speed blender and slowly blend until
completely smooth, then strain through a chinois. Combine 1 part demerara
syrup and 1 part pineapple rum to 2 parts pineapple juice. Store in an airtight
container, refrigerated, for up to 2 weeks.

specialty.name: Chicken Jus
specialty.recipe:
- 100 g canola oil
- 560 g diced onions (2 cm)
- 260 g peeled and diced carrots (2 cm)
- 260 g diced celery (2 cm)
- 100 g tomato paste
- 750 mL red wine
- 4.5 kg chicken wings
- 2.5 kg chicken feet
- 13.5 kg water
- 2 bay leaves
- 10 thyme sprigs
- 25 black peppercorns

Preheat a convection oven to 200°C/400°F, with the fan on high. In a large
roasting pan over high heat, warm the canola oil. Add the onions, carrots, and
celery and sauté until they caramelize, about 12 minutes. Add the tomato
paste and sauté until caramelized, 3 minutes more. Add the red wine and
reduce by half, about 10 minutes, then set aside. Spread the chicken wings in a
single layer on two large rimmed baking sheets and roast in the oven until
caramelized, about 50 minutes, rotating the pan once. Drain and discard any
rendered fat. Scrape the roasted wings into a large stockpot and add the
chicken feet and water. Bring to a simmer over medium heat, skimming the
stock of all impurities and fats that rise to the top. Add the vegetable mixture
to the stock, along with the bay leaves, thyme, and peppercorns. Turn the
heat to low and simmer, uncovered, for 6 hours, skimming every 30 minutes.
Strain the stock through a chinois, transfer to a large clean saucepan, and
continue to reduce over low heat until it is 1 kg. Strain the reduced jus
through a chinois and chill over an ice bath. Store in an airtight container,
refrigerated, for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 1 month.

MAKES 1 KG
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Cinnamon Orange Tea-Infused Sweet Vermouth
specialty.recipe:
- 3 heaping tbps cinnamon-orange tea
- 750 mL sweeth vermouth

Combine in a glass container for 1.5 hours, stirring occasionally, before straining through a coffee filter.

specialty.name: Coconut oil-washed bourbon
specialty.recipe:
- 6 oz unrefined coconut oil
- 750 mL bourbon

Combined coconut oil and bourbon in a glass container, shake to mix, and let infuse at room temperature for 6 hours. Transfer to freezer to solidify oil over night. Discard solids and strain bourbon through a coffee filter. 

Makes ~750 mL
Source: Tiki: Modern Tropical Cocktails

specialty.name: Coconut Syrup
specialty.recipe: 1:1 coconut water to cane sugar (or coconut sugar) (v/m)

specialty.name: Coffee Infused Angostura Bitters
specialty.recipe:
- 250 mL Cold Brew Coffee Concentrate
- 250 mL Angostura bitters

In a medium bowl, combine the coffee concentrate and Angostura bitters and
stir until fully incorporated. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up
to 1 month.

MAKES 500 ML
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Coffee Infused Dry Vermouth
specialty.recipe:
- 50 g whole coffee beans
- 500 mL Dry vermouth

Crush the coffee beans in a spice mill or coffee grinder until very coarsely
ground. Put them into an iSi canister. Add the vermouth and seal the canister.
Charge it twice using N2O (cream) chargers, shaking the canister between
each charge. Allow the canister to sit for 5 minutes and then vent by
squeezing the nozzle quickly; place a container underneath the tip to catch
any liquid that may be released. Unscrew the top of the canister. Once the
liquid stops bubbling, strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Let the liquid cool to room temperature and store in an airtight container,
refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 500 mL
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Corn Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 2.7 kg freeze-dried corn
- 2.8 kg sugar
- 3.8 L hot water
- Salt

In a high-speed blender, blitz the corn into a powder. Stir the sugar into the
corn and add the hot water. Let steep for 15 minutes, then strain through a
chinois and season with salt. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up
to 2 weeks.

MAKES 3.8 TO 4.7 L
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Cranberry Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 2 kg frozen cranberries
- 2 kg cranberry juice
- 500 g demerara sugar
Brix: 50

In a large saucepan, combine the cranberries and cranberry juice and bring to
a boil over high heat. Lower the heat and simmer until all of the cranberries
have popped. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it sit for 10
minutes. Strain the mixture through a chinois and cheesecloth or through a
superbag. Add the sugar and stir until fully dissolved. Let the syrup cool to
room temperature and store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1
month.

MAKES 1 KG
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Dill Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 150 g fresh dill
- 800 g Simple Syrup
Brix: 48

Add the dill and simple syrup to an iSi canister. Charge it twice using N2O
(cream) chargers, shaking the canister between each charge. Allow the canister to sit for 5 minutes and then vent by squeezing the nozzle quickly; place a container underneath the tip to catch any liquid that may be released.
Unscrew the top of the canister. Once the liquid stops bubbling, strain the
mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter. Let the syrup cool to room
temperature and store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 800 g
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Don's Remix
specialty.recipe:
- 125 g clarified grapefruit juice
- 125 g cane sugar
- 15 g grapefruit peel
- 7.5 g cinnamon bark, broken into pieces
- 2.625 g citric acid

Toast cinnamon bark in a small saucepan until just aromatic. Add the juice, peels, and sugar. Bring to a gentle simmer and cover for 15 minutes, stirring occassionaly and monitoring heat to avoid scorching. Let cool and string through a nut milk bag and then coffee filter. Stir in citric acid until dissolved. Makes 8 ounces.

See Tropical Standard p 150 for Don's Remix recipe.

specialty.name: Donn's Spices #2
specialty.description: Equal parts vanilla syrup and allspice dram
specialty.recipe: Combine equal parts vanilla syrup and allspice dram (St. Elizabeth’s). Stir to combine. Keeps refrigerated for several weeks.

specialty.name: Earl Grey Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 25 g Earl Grey tea
- 900 g hot water
- 800 g sugar
Brix: 48

Steep the tea in the hot water for 3 minutes. Strain the tea and add the sugar,
stirring until it’s fully dissolved. Let the syrup cool to room temperature and
store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 1.7 KG
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Earl Grey-Milk Washed Blanco Tequila
specialty.recipe:
- 25 g Earl Grey tea
- 750 mL Excellia Blanco tequila
- 15 mL lemon juice
- 100 mL whole milk

In an airtight jar, combine the tea and tequila, let steep for 20 minutes, and
then strain. In a medium saucepan over medium heat, combine the lemon
juice and milk and let simmer (do not boil) so the milk starts to curdle. Pour
the curdled mixture into the infused tequila, whisk well, and then let sit for 30
minutes. Strain the mixture through a superbag or a coffee filter. Store in
airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 2 months.

MAKES 750 ML
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Eucalyptus Bay Leaf Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 5 fresh bay leaves
- 1 L hot filtered water
- 1 kg sugar
-1 g eucalyptus oil

In a large container, steep the bay leaves in the hot water for 5 minutes. Add
the sugar and stir until fully dissolved. Stir in the eucalyptus oil (it is very
potent, be sure to weigh it precisely). Pass through a chinois and let cool to
room temperature. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1
month.

MAKES 1.5 
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Falernum
specialty.description: Tropical Standard/Sunken Harbor Club house falernum
specialty.recipe:
- 0.75 g star anise
- 0.75 g mace
- 1 g nutmeg, grated
- 1.5 g cloves
- 13 g lime peel
- 28 g ginger, thinly sliced
- 30 mL aged overproof rum (Hamilton 151)
- 125 mL unaged overproof Jamaican rum (Wray & Nephew)
- 0.6 mL almond extract
- 15 mL strained or clarified lemon juice
- 50 mL strained or clarified lime juice
- 265 mL 2:1 cane syrup
- 830 mL (estimated) 1:1 gum syrup

Grind dry spices with a mortal and pestle or spice grinder. Combine lime peel, ginger, and rums in a blender on high until ginger is liquefied and lime peel is thoroughly minced. 

Combine rum mixture and ground spices in a vacuum bag and seal. Place in a water bath at 125ºF for 12 hours or in the refrigerator for 24 hours. 

Finely strain mixture through a nutmilk bag, squeezing to maximize yield, and then pass through a coffee filter. Yield should be ~75%. 

Add almond extract, juices, and syrup. This should yield ~830 mL liquid. Combine with an equal volume of gum syrup.

MAKES 55 oz
Source: Tropical Standard (p. 232)

specialty.name: Falernum Bitters
specialty.description: Tropical Standard/Sunken Harbor Club house falernum
specialty.recipe:
- 0.75 g star anise
- 0.75 g mace
- 1 g nutmeg, grated
- 1.5 g cloves
- 13 g lime peel
- 28 g ginger, thinly sliced
- 30 mL aged overproof rum (Hamilton 151)
- 125 mL unaged overproof Jamaican rum (Wray & Nephew)

Grind dry spices with a mortal and pestle or spice grinder. Combine lime peel, ginger, and rums in a blender on high until ginger is liquefied and lime peel is thoroughly minced. 

Combine rum mixture and ground spices in a vacuum bag and seal. Place in a water bath at 125ºF for 12 hours or in the refrigerator for 24 hours. 

Finely strain mixture through a nutmilk bag, squeezing to maximize yield, and then pass through a coffee filter. Yield should be ~75%.

specialty.name: Fig Leaf Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 75 g fresh fig leaves
- 900 mL hot water
- 800 g sugar
Brix: 50

Rinse the fig leaves thoroughly under cool running water until the water runs
clear, then remove the stems and tear up the leaves. Steep the leaves and hot
water in a container for 10 to 15 minutes (taste as you go because it becomes
bitter very quickly). Strain through a chinois and stir in the sugar until it
dissolves. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 1.4 L
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Gardenia Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 1.8 kg clover honey
- 1.8 kg unsalted butter, cubed
- 140 g allspice dram
- 140 g Vanilla Syrup
- 310 g Cinnamon Syrup

In a medium saucepan over medium heat, warm the honey until it starts to
become liquid. (Be sure it does not get too hot or it will thicken.) Add the
butter and whisk until it is melted and the mixture is smooth. Let sit for 5
minutes, then whisk in the dram, vanilla syrup, and cinnamon syrup and let
cool. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 4 weeks.

MAKES 2 L
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Ginger Lime Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 600 g water
- 800 g light brown sugar
- 300 g ginger, chopped
- 20 g lime zest
-95 g lime juice
Brix: 50

In a medium saucepan over medium heat, combine the water, brown sugar,
and ginger and simmer for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove from the
heat and add the lime zest. Allow to steep for 30 minutes. Strain out the
ginger and lime zest and add the lime juice. Let the syrup cool to room
temperature and store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 1.5 KG
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Grapefruit Oleo Saccharum
specialty.recipe: Macerate equal mass grapefruit peels and cane sugar; let sit at room temperature, shaking occasionally, until sugar is completely dissolved' strain through nut milk bag or coffee filter to clarify

specialty.name: Grapefruit Punch Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 125 g clarified grapefruit juice
- 31.5 g grapefruit oleo saccharum
- 86.5 g cane sugar
- 1.5 g citric acid

Combine juice and oleo in blend on low, then slowly incorporate the sugar and acid. Makes 6 ounces.

See Tropical Standard p 216 for Grapefruit Punch Syrup Recipe.

specialty.name: Green Tea Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 90 g green tea
- 2.7 kg hot water
- 800 g sugar
- 3 g xanthan gum
- 30 g Arabic gum

In a large container, steep the tea in the hot water for 12 minutes. Pass the
liquid through a chinois, but do not to press the loose tea, as the liquid will be
bitter. Add the sugar and stir until fully dissolved, then transfer the mixture to
a high-speed blender. While blending at medium speed, sift in the xanthan
gum and then the Arabic gum. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for
up to 1 month.

MAKES 3.8 L
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Green Tea Yogurt Syrup
specialty.recipe: Strained full-fat yogurt sweetened with green tea–infused 2:1 simple syrup

specialty.name: Green Tea Yogurt Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 500 mL Green Tea Syrup (preceding recipe)
- 500 mL unsweetened sheep’s milk yogurt

In a medium bowl, combine the tea syrup and yogurt and stir until fully
incorporated. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 1 L
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Grenadine
specialty.recipe:
- 500 g  Pomegranate juice
- 450 g turbinado sugar
- 225 g pomegranate molasses
- 10 orange twists
Brix: 55

Warm the pomegranate juice and sugar in a saucepan over medium heat. Stir until the sugar completely dissolves, making sure to never bring the juice to a boil. Remove from the heat and add the pomegranate molasses. Express the oils of the orange twists into the mixture and stir. Let the syrup cool to room temperature and store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 2 weeks.

MAKES 1.1 KG
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Guava Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 1 L filtered water
- 2 kg guava puree
- Sugar as needed
Brix: 50

In a medium container, whisk together the water and guava puree. Stir in the
sugar to taste. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 2 weeks.

MAKES 4.7 L
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Horseradish Tincture
specialty.recipe:
- 150 g horseradish, washed, peeled, and diced
- 750 mL vodka

In a large bowl, combine the horseradish and vodka and let steep for 10
minutes, then strain through a chinois. Store in an airtight container at room
temperature indefinitely.

MAKES 750 ML
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Horseradish-Infused Gin
specialty.recipe:
- 150 g horseradish, washed, peeled, and sliced lengthwise
- 750 mL Plymouth gin

Combine the horseradish and gin in a bag and vacuum seal. Steam in the
combi oven for 30 minutes at 60°C/140°F and 100 percent humidity, then
strain through a chinois. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 6
weeks.

MAKES 750 ML
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Jalapeño Infused Agave Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 3 medium jalapeños
- 250 g hot water
- 810 g organic light blue agave nectar

Dice the jalapeños, retaining all the seeds, and steep in the hot water for 3
minutes. Taste the mixture to ensure that the spice level is to your taste.
Allow them to steep longer for a spicier end product. Strain out the jalapeños
when the desired spice level has been reached and stir the agave nectar into the jalapeño-infused water until it’s fully integrated. Let the syrup cool to room temperature and store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1
month.

MAKES 1.1 kg
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Jalapeño Infused Tequila
specialty.recipe:
- 5 medium jalapeños, diced
- 750 ml Blanco tequila

Steep the jalapeños and tequila in a container for 5 minutes. Taste the mixture
to ensure that the spice level is to your taste. Allow to steep longer for a
spicier end product. Strain out the jalapeños when the desired spice level has
been reached. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, indefinitely.

MAKES 750 ML
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Kabocha Squash Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 800 g Kabocha Squash Water (following recipe)
- 800 g demerara sugar
Brix: 50

In a saucepan over medium heat, heat the squash water until it’
s simmering. Remove from the heat and stir the sugar into the water until fully dissolved. Let the syrup cool to room temperature and store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 1.6 KG
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Kabocha Squash Water
specialty.recipe:
- 1 kabocha squash, quartered and seeded
- 1.6 kg water
- 6 g salt
- 15 g whole allspice
- 100 g Ceylon cinnamon sticks, crushed

Preheat the oven to 200°C/400°F. Place the squash on a sheet pan and roast
for 1½ hours. When cool enough to handle, scoop out the flesh of the squash,
discarding the rest. Measure out 900 g of the roasted squash and add to a
saucepan along with the water, salt, allspice, and cinnamon. Boil over high
heat for 10 minutes. Strain the mixture though a chinois. Let the water cool
to room temperature and store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to
1 week.

MAKES 1 KG
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Kaffir Lime Leaf-Infused Rum
specialty.recipe:
- 250 mL unaged or lightly aged rum (1:1 blend of Wray & Nephew and Pere Labat 59º)
- 6–8 fresh lime leaves (~3–4 g total), rough chopped

Add rum and torn leaves to 500 mL iSi and double charge, shaking in between charges. Let rest 1–2 minutes, swirling occasionally. Vent rapidly and strain liquid through a coffee filter.

*Add 7.5 g sliced galangal, 10 g sliced lemongrass, 2 Thai chiles, and one small (2 g) dried shiitake mushroom to make Tom Kha Infused-Rum

specialty.name: Lapsang Souchong-Infused Crème de Cacao
specialty.recipe:
- 30 g Lapsang Souchong black tea
- 1 L  Crème de cacao

Combine the tea and crème de cacao in a container and let steep for 45
minutes, then strain the mixture through a chinois. Store in an airtight
container, refrigerated, indefinitely.

MAKES 1 L
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Lavender Honey Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 10 g dried lavender flowers
- 350 g clover honey
- 225 g hot water
Brix: 60

Stir the lavender flowers and honey into the hot water until the honey is fully
dissolved. Let the syrup cool to room temperature and store in an airtight
container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 575 g
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Lemon Verbena Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 32 g dried lemon verbena
- 900 g hot water
- 800 g sugar
Brix: 48

Steep the lemon verbena in the hot water for 5 minutes. Strain the mixture and stir in the sugar until it’ s fully dissolved. Let the syrup cool to room temperature and store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 1.7 kg
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Lemon Verbena–Infused Buttermilk
specialty.recipe:
- 500 mL Lemon Verbena Syrup (following recipe)
- 500 mL buttermilk

In a medium bowl, combine the syrup and buttermilk and stir until fully
incorporated. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 1 L
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Lemongrass syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 4 stalks fresh lemongrass (bottom 4–5 inches only — discard the woody tops and the dry outer leaves)
- 170 g white or cane sugar
- 170 mL water
- 1.5 g citric acid (~0.5% of finished syrup)
- 0.5 g salt (~0.2%)

Trim the bulb end and outer dry leaves off each lemongrass stalk. Bash the tender inner core with the back of a knife or a pestle until it splinters and the cell walls rupture and slice into ~1 cm rounds. 

Combine sugar and water in a small saucepan. Warm to ~75°C, swirling until fully dissolved. Do not boil. Pull from heat. Add lemongrass. Hold at 70–75°C for 15–20 minutes (sous vide or a low-low burner with a thermometer).

Cover and transfer to the fridge for an 8–12 hour cold steep. This step does most of the aromatic extraction.

Strain through a fine mesh, then through a coffee filter or superbag for clarity. Stir in citric acid and salt.

MAKES 250 mL
Source: Claude

specialty.name: Lime Cordial
specialty.recipe:
- 125 g clarified lime juice
- 125 g white or cane sugar
- 6.25 g lime peel
- 1.25 g mali acid
- 2.5 g citric acid

Combine juice, sugar, and peel in a saucepan. Bring to a simmer, stirring occasionally. Once at a simmer, cover and reduce heat to low. After 15 minutes, remove from heat and let sit for 6 hours. Strain through a coffee filter and then add acids.

MAKES 8 oz
Source: Tropical Standard (p. 221)

specialty.name: Lime oleo saccharum
specialty.recipe: Macerate equal mass lime peels and cane sugar; let sit at room temperature, shaking occasionally, until sugar is completely dissolved' strain through nut milk bag or coffee filter to clarify

specialty.name: Maple Shrub
specialty.description: 1:1 maple syrup to apple cider vinegar (v/v)

specialty.name: Mint-Infused Bourbon
specialty.recipe:
- 35 g mint leaves
- 750 mL Old Forester 86 bourbon

Combine the mint and bourbon in an iSi canister. Charge it twice using N2O (cream) chargers, shaking the canister between each charge. Allow the canister to sit for 5 minutes and then vent by pushing the nozzle out quickly; place a container underneath the tip to catch any liquid that may be released. Unscrew the top of the canister. Once the liquid stops bubbling, strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, indefinitely.

MAKES 750 mL
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Mushroom Broth
specialty.recipe:
- 100 g dried shiitake mushrooms
- 1 L hot water

In a large bowl, combine the mushrooms and hot water and let steep for 30 minutes. Strain the mixture through a coffee filter and let come to room temperature. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for up to 1 week.

MAKES 1 L
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Passionfruit Cordial
specialty.recipe:
- 125 g clarified passionfruit puree
- 12.5 g acacia gum
- 91.5 g white or cane sugar
-  1.75 g citric acid

Place clarified puree in a blender on low and slowly add the acacia gum until fully dissolved (a small head will form). Continue to blend and slowly add the sugar, then acid. 

MAKES 5 oz
Source: Tropical Standard (p. 229)

specialty.name: Passionfruit Syrup
specialty.recipe: 1:1 passionfruit purée to 2:1 simple syrup by volume; strain through coffee filter to clarify

specialty.name: Pineapple Gum (Gomme) Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 125 g finely strained or clarified pineapple juice
- 12.5 g acacia gum (gum arabic)
- 87.5 g sugar of choice
- 0.5 g citric acid

Blend pineapple juice on low and slowly incorporate acacia gum; speed can be increased but try to limit foam production. Return to low speed and incorporate sugar and acid. Makes 6 oz.

See Tropical Standard p 197 for Gum Syrups.

specialty.name: Rhubarb Shrub
specialty.recipe:
- 40 g salt
- 200 g sugar
- 1 kg white balsamic vinegar
- 1.02 kg water
- 950 g rhubarb, washed and cut into 1-inch pieces
- 5 drops red food coloring

In a large bowl, combine the salt, sugar, vinegar, and water and stir to make a
brine. Combine the brine and rhubarb in a bag and vacuum seal. Place the bag
in a sheet pan and steam in the combi oven for 15 minutes at 63°C/145°F and
100 percent humidity. Transfer the bag to the refrigerator and let chill, then
strain the liquid through a chinois and stir in the food coloring. Store in an
airtight container, refrigerated, indefinitely.

MAKES 2.5 L
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Rice-Infused Coconut Water
specialty.recipe:
- 475 mL coconut water
- 1/2 cup red, brown, or basmati rice

Combine in a glass container and refrigerate overnight. Strain through a coffee filter.

Source: Tiki: Modern Tropical Cocktails

specialty.name: Sarsaparilla Tincture/Bitters
specialty.recipe:
Dried sarsaparilla root steeped in high-proof neutral spirit for 2 weeks; strain and dilute to taste.

Bittercube Sarsaparilla bitters are an easier options.

specialty.name: Spiced molasses syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 100 mL water
- 100 g sugar
- 1–2 tbsp molasses
- 1 start anise
- 1 clove
- 1 cinnamon stick
- 1 mace aril

Combine everything in a small saucepan on low and bring to a simmer. Simmer until desired spiciness, let cool, and strain through a coffee filter.

specialty.name: Spiced Tomato Water
specialty.recipe: Strained juice of ripe tomatoes with salt, white pepper, celery seed and hot sauce; chill overnight

specialty.name: Spicy Ginger Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 250 g ginger juice
- 250 g turbinado sugar
Brix: 50

To make the juice, run whole stalks of ginger through an auger or masticating
juicer. Strain the juice through a chinois and cheesecloth or through a
superbag, straining out the lighter colored starches that remain on the bottom
of the container. Warm the juice in a medium saucepan over medium heat
until it’s just under a simmer. Add the sugar and stir to dissolve. Let the syrup
cool to room temperature and store in an airtight container, refrigerated, for
up to 2 weeks.

MAKES 500 g
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Tellicherry Black Pepper Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 150 g Tellicherry black pepper, coarsely ground
- 800 g Demerara Syrup

Combine the pepper and simple syrup in an iSi canister. Charge it twice using
N2O (cream) chargers, shaking the canister between each charge. Allow the
canister to sit for 5 minutes and then vent by pushing the nozzle out quickly;
place a container underneath the tip to catch any liquid that may be released.
Unscrew the top of the canister. Once the liquid stops bubbling, strain the
mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter. Store in an airtight container,
refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 800 g
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Thai Bird Chile–Infused Aperol
specialty.recipe:
- 10 Thai bird chiles, diced
- 750 mL Aperol

Steep the chiles and Aperol in a container for 5 minutes. Taste the mixture to
ensure that the spice level is to your liking. Allow to steep longer for a spicier
end product. Strain out the chiles when the desired spice level has been
reached. Store in an airtight container, refrigerated, indefinitely.

MAKES 750 ML
Source: NoMad

specialty.name: Vanilla Syrup
specialty.recipe:
- 800 g Simple Syrup (this page)
- 3 vanilla beans, split

Combine the simple syrup and vanilla in an iSi canister. Charge it twice using
N2O (cream) chargers, shaking the canister between each charge. Allow the
canister to sit for 5 minutes and then vent by pushing the nozzle out quickly;
place a container underneath the tip to catch any liquid that may be released.
Unscrew the top of the canister. Once the liquid stops bubbling, strain the
mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter. Store in an airtight container,
refrigerated, for up to 1 month.

MAKES 800 g
Source: NoMad
